milestone: Decoration Shapes -- basic-geometry vocabulary + independent fill/outline transparency
slice: Direct follow-up feedback on the Decoration Shapes sprint (Build 0065): add all the basic geometry shapes, and add transparency to both fill colour and outline colour. SHAPE_KINDS grew from 5 to 14 -- Rectangle/Triangle/Diamond/Pentagon/Hexagon/Octagon/Cross/Trapezoid/Parallelogram joined the original Circle/Star/Arrow/Speech Bubble/Banner. engineRuntime.js gained a shared _regularPolygonPath helper (one N-sided-polygon routine reused for Triangle/Pentagon/Hexagon/Octagon) plus straightforward path geometry for the rest; renderer/slideRenderer.js's _layerDrawShape got the identical geometry added by hand, in lockstep, since the real Reader-facing renderer has no dependency on any Builder module. Two new fields, graphicFillOpacity/graphicStrokeOpacity (each 0..1, defaulting to 1 so every pre-existing Shape renders unchanged), ride through the same mirrored-Layer/Engine-Adapter/Build-convergence pipeline the original sprint built -- no second pipeline; _drawShape composes each against the Layer's own overall opacity (multiplied, not replaced) via two separate fill()/stroke() calls each with their own globalAlpha. The Inspector gained Fill Opacity / Outline Opacity sliders paired beside their Colour pickers. Verified end-to-end via Playwright: all 14 shapes have exactly one real tile; a Hexagon's Fill/Outline Opacity persist independently to both the Experience and its mirrored Layer; a real ProjectCompiler Build compiles the correct fillOpacity/strokeOpacity into the published Theme; a pixel-sampled before/after (30% vs 100% fill opacity) shows a real, visibly different rendered colour. goldenBuild.js 30/30 unchanged.
